title: Kailadevi Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Kailadevi Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Rajasthan

overview.short: Kailadevi Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected wildlife area in Karauli district of Rajasthan, India.
overview.description: Kailadevi Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected wildlife area in Karauli district of Rajasthan, India. It was notified in 1983 and covers approximately 676.38 km² area. The sanctuary forms the northern extension of the Ranthambore Tiger Reserve and serves as a wildlife corridor between Ranthambore and the Chambal River region.

topPlaces.name: Kailadevi
topPlaces.description: Kailadevi is a village in Karauli Tehsil, in the Karauli District of the Indian state of Rajasthan. It falls under the Bharatpur Division.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kailadevi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kailadevi Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kailadevi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jaipur International Airport (126 km) and the nearest railway station is Gwalior Junction (124 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kailadevi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kailadevi Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kailadevi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Kailadevi Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kailadevi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kaila Devi Temple, Kailadevi.
